title: quamoclit acutangula
intro: Quamoclit acutangula, also known as the "trap-jaw" or "Climbing Mallow," is a plant species in the family Malvaceae, native to tropical Asia and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Quamoclit acutangula include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema. Quamoclit acutangula is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
